message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  I consider a simple, deterministic SEIR-like model without spatial or age structure, including a presymptomatic state and distinguishing between reported and nonreported infected individuals. Using a time-dependent contagion factor
                  <jats:italic>β</jats:italic>
                  (
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  ) (in the form a piecewise constant function) and literature values for other epidemiological parameters, I obtain good fits to observational data for the cumulative number of confirmed cases in over 160 regions (103 countries, 24 Brazilian states and 34 U.S. counties). The evolution of
                  <jats:italic>β</jats:italic>
                  is useful for characterizing the state of the epidemic. The analysis provides insight into general trends associated with the pandemic, such as the tendency toward reduced contagion, and the fraction of the population exposed to the virus.
                </jats:p>
title: A SEIR-like model with a time-dependent contagion factor describes the dynamics of the Covid-19 pandemic

reference.unstructured: D. Karlen , “Characterizing the spread of CoViD-19,” e-print (2020): arxiv.org/abs/2007.07156.

H. M. Paiva , R. J. M. Afonso , I. L. de Oliveira , and G. F. Garcia , “A data-driven model to describe and forecast the dynamics of COVID-19 transmission,” PLoS ONE 15, e0236386. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0236386.
G. Gaeta , “A simple SIR model with a large set of asymptomatic infectives,” e-print (2020): arXiv:2003.08720.
A. G. M. Neves and G. Guerrero , “Predicting the evolution of the COVID-19 epidemic with the A-SIR model: Lombardy, Italy and Sao Paulo state, Brazil,” e-print (2020): arXiv:2005.11182.
D. J. Daley and J. Gani , Epidemic Modelling: An Introduction (Cambridge University Press, Cambridge, U.K., 1999).
O. Diekmann and J. A. P. Heesterbeek , Mathematical Epidemiology of Infectious Diseases: Model Building, Analysis and Interpretation (Wiley, Chichester, 2000).
J. Murray , Mathematical Biology: I (Springer, New York, 2011).
W. H. Press et al., Numerical Recipes (Cambridge University Press, Cambridge, U.K., 1986).

A numerical method for some stochastic differential equations with multiplicative noise
